kernel: platform -> company -> business -> carrier -> fact -> projection
policy: A new platform business/carrier table is allowed only when a family has high pressure that cannot be solved by registry metadata or optional carrier fields.

family_carrier_fits.decision: Keep at platform/company level; do not bind to project.
family_carrier_fits.next_action: Use as policy and capability context for future business/carrier scopes.

family_carrier_fits.decision: Keep identity native and reusable across businesses and carriers.
family_carrier_fits.next_action: Do not add project-specific ownership to counterparty identity.

family_carrier_fits.decision: Keep project as the construction carrier, not the platform kernel.
family_carrier_fits.next_action: Expose project through the carrier contract and avoid promoting project-only semantics to platform.

family_carrier_fits.decision: Keep product/material identity reusable at company and industry identity level.
family_carrier_fits.next_action: Separate identity from material lifecycle facts in later standards.

family_carrier_fits.current_binding: tender.bid currently requires project_id; income contracts usually bind to project
family_carrier_fits.decision: Treat tender as the first explicit business-before-carrier pressure point.
family_carrier_fits.next_action: Design optional business/carrier metadata before changing tender or contract schema.

family_carrier_fits.current_binding: project.project through construction expense contracts and sc.general.contract
family_carrier_fits.decision: Keep project-primary for construction expense, but preserve general-contract exception pressure.
family_carrier_fits.next_action: Split general company spending from project expense before any platform table decision.

family_carrier_fits.current_binding: project.project as normal payment and approval carrier
family_carrier_fits.decision: Keep payment facts project-carried while the expense business remains construction-specific.
family_carrier_fits.next_action: Revisit only if company-level payment workflows appear outside project or contract.

family_carrier_fits.family: receipt income invoice and tax realization
family_carrier_fits.decision: Keep income realization project-carried in construction.
family_carrier_fits.next_action: Add business-level aggregation only if income lifecycle must span multiple project carriers.

family_carrier_fits.current_binding: company-level fund account with optional project and project-carried reconciliation
family_carrier_fits.decision: Keep account master company-level and reconciliation carrier-aware.
family_carrier_fits.next_action: Do not collapse account, reconciliation, and ledger into one business fact.

family_carrier_fits.family: project budget BOQ and cost control
family_carrier_fits.decision: Keep as construction project control, not platform business core.
family_carrier_fits.next_action: Use as project-carrier exemplar for future carrier contract tests.

family_carrier_fits.decision: Keep material lifecycle project-carried in construction.
family_carrier_fits.next_action: Preserve product identity boundary and avoid making projections writable.
Do not fold professional lifecycle models back into generic contract or payment tables.

family_carrier_fits.family: progress quality safety risk and diary
family_carrier_fits.decision: Keep as construction project execution evidence.
family_carrier_fits.next_action: Use these facts for visibility but keep projections derived.

family_carrier_fits.family: document admin payroll and office operations
family_carrier_fits.current_binding: company workflow with optional project anchors
family_carrier_fits.next_action: Promote to business fact only when documents own income/expense amount semantics.

family_carrier_fits.decision: Keep read-side derived; never use projection as source-of-truth.
family_carrier_fits.next_action: Add business-level projections only after fact-side business/carrier pressure is proven.

family_carrier_fits.current_binding: source-fidelity replay carriers and target anchors
family_carrier_fits.decision: Keep legacy evidence out of platform model definition.
family_carrier_fits.next_action: Use replay evidence to validate target models, not to define the hierarchy.

family_carrier_fits.family: workflow approval dictionary audit and validation
family_carrier_fits.decision: Keep as policy over facts, not fact ownership.
family_carrier_fits.next_action: Prepare scope vocabulary for company/business/carrier policy application.

family_carrier_fits.family: scene capability subscription and frontend contract runtime
family_carrier_fits.next_action: Expose carrier context to frontend contracts without embedding business-specific rendering.

family_carrier_fits.current_binding: native Odoo/OCA models with compatibility hooks
family_carrier_fits.next_action: Do not let technical extensions become business source-of-truth.
